nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Haha! Young friend, I see you have the makings of a Go player.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Would you like to learn some Go skills from me?

options.text: Senior, I want to learn 'Mighty Dragon Repents'
Senior, I'd like to challenge you again
I want to learn about Go first

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Go, black and white stones, 19 lines crisscrossing. It looks simple but the variations are endless. Just like martial arts, it's all about momentum and strategy.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Let's start with a 9x9 board. Once you get the hang of it, we'll move to 13x13 and 19x19.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Good! I like your spirit! Besides the Eighteen Dragon-Subduing Palms, Go is what I do best.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: But here's my rule: if you want to learn, you must prove yourself first! Beat me in a game, and I'll teach you 'Mighty Dragon Repents'.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Good! Let me see what you've got!

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Haha! Excellent! I knew I wasn't wrong about you. Let me teach you 'Mighty Dragon Repents'.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Everyone makes mistakes in Go. This move gives you a chance to take back one move. But remember the rules: only once per game!

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Now go find Linghu Chong at Shaolin Temple. His Dugu Nine Swords technique is quite something.

options.text: I'd like to hear more of your teachings
Senior, may I challenge you again?

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Haha! Losing is no big deal. It's all about improving your skills. Want to try again?

options.text: Yes! Please let me try again, Senior!

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: So you learned the Dugu Nine Swords? Linghu Chong's swordsmanship is extraordinary. The martial arts world needs more people like you.

options.text: Thank you for your guidance, Senior!
May I ask about the Eighteen Dragon-Subduing Palms?

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: You've completed all the main quests! Xiangyang needs someone like you. But there's always more to learn in the martial arts world. Want to compete with me again?

options.text: I need to consolidate what I've learned

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Ready for another game? Playing more will improve your skills!

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Playing Go is like commanding an army - you must know when to advance and retreat, understand what's real and what's feint. Remember: corners are gold, edges are silver, center is grass. Don't rush to the middle at the start.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: No worries! I'm not going anywhere. Come find me when you're ready.

nodes.speaker: Hong Qigong
nodes.text: Off you go! I need to find something to eat. The beggar's chicken here at Huashan is the best!
